title: Tents
intro: Tents is a logic puzzle in which the goal is to place tents next to trees subject to a number of constraints.
board_example: Below is a 4x4 example board with 4 trees:
rule_pairing: Each tree must be paired with exactly one tent, placed horizontally or vertically adjacent. A tree may have multiple tents next to it, but in the final solution only one of them forms its unique pair.
example_pairing: For example, the middle tree in the second row touches two tents, but each actually belongs to the tree below it.
rule_no_touch: Tents must not touch each other, including diagonally. Once you place a tent, cells horizontally, vertically, and diagonally adjacent to it must be tent-free.
example_invalid: For instance, the configuration of tents below is invalid because the two tents touch each other diagonally:
rule_counts: The numbers on the edges indicate exactly how many tents are in that row or column.
example_row: For instance, the first row has a 2, requiring two tents, while the second row has 0, so it contains no tents.
example_col: The first column has 1, so there will be exactly one tent in that column.
solved: The game is solved when you've placed tents equal to the number of trees, following the rules above.
solved_board: Below is an example of a solved board:
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:
control_mark_free: Marking the cell as tent-free

compact.rule_pairing: Each tree must be paired with exactly one tent (), placed next to it horizontally or vertically.
compact.rule_no_touch: Tents never touch each other (including diagonally).
compact.rule_counts: Numbers indicate exactly how many tents go into that row/column.
